hero.headline: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA in Operations Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A accredited | Don Bosco/Salesian educational network

sections.tldr: Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA in Operations Management builds skills in operations strategy, production planning, quality management, and lean principles. The programme includes Startup Management as a common Sem 3 core subject, plus Business Policy and Strategic Management and a Research Project/Dissertation in Sem 4. NAAC A accredited, UGC-DEB approved. Part of the Don Bosco/Salesian educational network. Fee confirmed at admission.

about.body:
Assam Don Bosco University (CDOE) is a private university in Guwahati, Assam, operating under the globally recognized Don Bosco/Salesian educational network. The Salesian order runs more than 1,500 educational institutions across 130+ countries. ADB holds NAAC A accreditation and UGC-DEB approval for its online programmes.

The Operations Management specialisation focuses on operations strategy, production planning, quality management, and lean principles. The curriculum prepares graduates for roles in manufacturing, logistics, supply chain, and process improvement across industries.

Startup Management appears as a common Sem 3 core subject across all ADB MBA specialisations. For operations professionals, this builds skills in setting up production systems, designing lean processes for early-stage companies, and managing startup operations from day one.

Northeast India has a growing manufacturing and logistics sector. ADB's Operations Management programme aligns with the region's industrial development, including food processing, tea manufacturing, and emerging light manufacturing clusters in Assam.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Detailed Sem 3-4 curriculum for ADB's Operations Management specialisation is confirmed each admission cycle. The course mix emphasizes operations strategy, production planning, quality management, and lean principles. Our counsellor can share the current Sem 3-4 subject list for this specialisation within 24 hours.

whoHires.body:
ADB MBA Operations Management graduates target roles across manufacturing, logistics, quality, and process improvement:

**Industries:** Manufacturing (food processing, tea, textiles), logistics and warehousing, supply chain management, quality assurance, pharmaceutical operations, FMCG production, and government/PSU operations

**Entry-level roles:** Operations Executive, Production Supervisor, Quality Analyst, Supply Chain Coordinator, Inventory Controller, Process Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** Operations Manager, Production Manager, Quality Manager, Logistics Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Lean Six Sigma Lead

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations, Plant Head, Director of Manufacturing, COO, Head of Supply Chain, Director of Quality

**Employers:** Food processing companies, tea manufacturing firms (Assam), logistics companies, FMCG manufacturing units, pharmaceutical production, and NE India industrial clusters.

**Note:** NE India's food processing and tea manufacturing sectors create steady demand for operations management professionals. ADB's location in Guwahati provides access to the region's largest commercial hub.

skills.body:
The Operations Management specialisation builds skills across production planning, quality systems, and process optimization:

**Technical skills:**
- Production Planning: demand forecasting, capacity planning, MRP/MRP-II concepts, production scheduling, and shop floor control
- Quality Management: TQM principles, ISO 9001 framework, statistical process control, quality audit methodology, and cost of quality analysis
- Lean Operations: value stream mapping, 5S implementation, waste elimination, Kaizen methodology, and continuous improvement frameworks
- Supply Chain: procurement strategy, vendor management, inventory optimization, distribution planning, and logistics cost analysis

**Applied skills:**
- Process Improvement: bottleneck identification, cycle time reduction, OEE measurement, and process redesign projects
- Startup Operations: production system setup for startups, lean operations design, and resource allocation for early-stage companies

**Soft skills:** Cross-functional team leadership, supplier negotiation, presenting operations metrics to leadership, and managing change during process improvement projects.

comparisons.body:
Comparing ADB Online MBA Operations Management against peer programmes:

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA Operations (Rs 94,000, NAAC A+):** Uttaranchal offers 8 specialisations with NAAC A+ at a fixed Rs 94,000. ADB brings Don Bosco/Salesian heritage and Startup Management as a mandatory core. Uttaranchal wins on fee transparency and NAAC grade. ADB wins on global educational network recognition.

**vs Sharda Online MBA Operations (Rs 1,54,500, NAAC A+):** Sharda is significantly more expensive with NCR positioning and proximity to Noida manufacturing. ADB offers Don Bosco heritage at a likely lower fee point. Sharda wins on NCR industrial corridor access. ADB wins on Salesian network and NE India positioning.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA Operations (Rs 86,400, NAAC A+):** Galgotias is budget-tier with NCR location and proximity to Greater Noida industrial zone. ADB offers Don Bosco heritage and NE India regional focus. Galgotias wins on published fee and NCR brand. ADB wins on global network recognition.

**vs Vignan Online MBA Operations (Rs 90,000, NAAC A+):** Vignan offers a flat Rs 90,000 fee with South India manufacturing hub (Guntur/AP) access. ADB offers Don Bosco heritage and NE India positioning. Both are budget-tier. Choose based on regional preference and whether Don Bosco network value matters.

fees.body:
The Assam Don Bosco University Online MBA in Operations Management fee structure is confirmed at admission. Please reconfirm current fees, payment options, and any applicable charges with our counsellor before enrolment.

Assam Don Bosco University is a UGC-DEB approved NAAC A accredited institution in Guwahati, part of the globally recognized Don Bosco/Salesian educational network. Financing options including zero-cost EMI may be available. Confirm terms with counsellor.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with ADB University each quarter. Fees listed on our pages are indicative pending verification each admission cycle.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Operations Management as the specialisation, issued by Assam Don Bosco University under UGC-DEB online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. The university is part of the Don Bosco/Salesian educational network with global recognition across 130+ countries. Valid for government recruitment, PSU operations roles, manufacturing management positions, and supply chain certifications eligibility.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from ADB Online MBA Operations Management students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Sanjib
items.liked: Don Bosco educational network gives the degree recognition beyond NE India. Operations curriculum covers production planning and quality management well
items.disliked: Fee is not publicly listed on the website. Required contacting admissions for exact details
items.body: I chose ADB because the Don Bosco name carries weight in NE India and internationally. The operations curriculum covers what I need for my manufacturing role. Startup Management as a core subject was useful since I plan to set up a food processing unit in Assam. The dissertation in Sem 4 required structured research, which added academic depth.

items.name: Pallavi
items.liked: NE India's only NAAC A university offering a dedicated online MBA in Operations Management. Lean principles and quality management topics were practical
items.disliked: Some study materials could include more NE India industry case studies. Faculty interaction timing could be more flexible
items.body: Working in logistics in Nagaland, I needed an online MBA from a recognized NE India institution. ADB fit perfectly. The operations subjects covered production planning and process optimization at a level applicable to my daily work. The Don Bosco brand is well-known across the Northeast, which helps professionally.

items.name: Arjun
items.liked: Startup Management as a mandatory core is unusual and genuinely useful for operations professionals thinking about entrepreneurship
items.disliked: Brand recognition outside NE India is limited. LMS interface needs modernization for a smoother learning experience
items.body: The operations curriculum is functional and covers the key areas. Quality management, lean principles, and production planning are all addressed. The trade-off is brand recognition outside the Northeast. In NE India, Don Bosco is strong. Nationally, the brand is still growing. For operations roles in the region, ADB works well.

faqs.question: What is Assam Don Bosco University's affiliation?
faqs.answer: Assam Don Bosco University is part of the Don Bosco/Salesian educational network, a globally recognized Catholic educational order operating 1,500+ institutions across 130+ countries. The university is a private institution in Guwahati, Assam.

faqs.question: Is ADB Online MBA UGC-DEB approved?
faqs.answer: Yes. Assam Don Bosco University holds UGC-DEB approval for its online programmes. The MBA degree is legally equivalent to a campus MBA under UGC 2020 guidelines.

faqs.question: Why is the fee structure confirmed at admission?
faqs.answer: ADB's fee structure varies by admission cycle and payment plan. The exact fee, semester breakdown, and any applicable charges are confirmed during the admission process. Contact our counsellor for the current cycle's fee details.

faqs.question: What operations roles can I target after this MBA?
faqs.answer: Operations Manager, Production Manager, Quality Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Logistics Manager, and Plant Head are common roles. NE India's food processing, tea manufacturing, and logistics sectors provide regional opportunities.

faqs.question: Is the detailed Sem 3-4 syllabus available?
faqs.answer: The detailed Sem 3-4 subject list for Operations Management is confirmed each admission cycle. Our counsellor can share the current semester-wise subject breakdown within 24 hours. Contact us for the latest curriculum.

chooseIf: Don Bosco/Salesian educational heritage and global network recognition matter to you.
You are based in NE India and want a NAAC A accredited local university with UGC-DEB approval.
Startup Management as a mandatory core subject adds value to your operations career plan.
NE India's food processing, tea manufacturing, or logistics sectors are your target industries.
consider: Uttaranchal at Rs 94,000 with NAAC A+ offers a clear published fee and 8 specialisations.
Galgotias at Rs 86,400 with NAAC A+ provides NCR brand positioning at a lower published fee.
Vignan at Rs 90,000 with NAAC A+ offers a fixed published fee with South India recognition.
If national brand recognition is your top priority, Amity, NMIMS, or MUJ have stronger pan-India positioning.
